title: Embrace the Cringe: National Awkward Moments Day
story: Everyone has those moments when they wave back at someone who wasn't waving at them, or call their teacher "Mom" by accident! National Awkward Moments Day on March 18 celebrates all those cringe-worthy times that make us want to hide under a rock. People share their most embarrassing stories on social media, laugh about awkward encounters, and realize everyone has these moments. Some folks even purposely do silly things like wearing mismatched socks or telling dad jokes to embrace the awkwardness. The holiday reminds us that being awkward is totally normal and actually pretty funny when we look back on it!
activity_question: What do people do on National Awkward Moments Day?
activity_choices: Share embarrassing stories and laugh about awkward times
Hide under blankets all day to avoid people
activity_answer: Share embarrassing stories and laugh about awkward times
follow_up_question: What's the most awkward moment you've ever experienced?

title: Fuel Made From French Fries!
story: Cars can run on fuel made from cooking oil! National Biodiesel Day celebrates this amazing green fuel every March 18. People visit biodiesel stations, learn about renewable energy, and discover how restaurants' used cooking oil gets turned into car fuel. Some families even convert their own vehicles to run on biodiesel, making their cars smell like french fries when they drive around town!
activity_question: What can be used to make biodiesel fuel?
bonus_fact: Some biodiesel cars actually smell like whatever food was cooked in the oil!

title: Lacy Cookies That Crumble and Crunch
story: Every March 18, cookie lovers celebrate National Lacy Oatmeal Cookie Day by baking the thinnest, crispiest cookies ever! These special treats are completely different from thick, chewy oatmeal cookies. Lacy oatmeal cookies spread super thin while baking, creating delicate, see-through treats that look like golden doilies. People love how they practically melt on your tongue with their buttery, caramel-like flavor. Bakers often roll them into tubes while they're still warm or drape them over bowls to make edible cookie cups for ice cream!
activity_question: What makes lacy oatmeal cookies different from regular oatmeal cookies?
bonus_fact: Some bakers use lacy cookies as edible bowls for desserts!

title: Messy, Delicious Sloppy Joe Day!
story: Get ready to make a mess because March 18 is National Sloppy Joe Day! This wonderfully messy sandwich gets celebrated by families firing up their stoves to cook ground beef in tangy tomato sauce. People pile the saucy mixture high on hamburger buns, grab extra napkins, and dig in. Restaurants join the fun by offering special sloppy joe deals and creative twists like turkey or veggie versions. Social media fills with photos of people's messiest, most delicious creations. Some families even have contests to see who can eat their sloppy joe with the fewest napkins - spoiler alert: everyone loses that challenge!
activity_question: What do people need lots of when eating sloppy joes?
bonus_fact: The sloppy joe sandwich was invented in the 1930s in Iowa!

title: National Inane Answering Message Day Gets Silly
story: Your phone's voicemail greeting probably sounds pretty normal, but on March 18, people celebrate National Inane Answering Message Day by recording the goofiest messages possible! Instead of "Hi, leave a message," folks get creative with silly voices, weird sound effects, or completely ridiculous greetings. Some people pretend to be robots, sing opera, or act like they're actually answering the phone but can't hear you. It's a fun way to make friends and family smile when they call!
activity_question: What do people do differently with their voicemail on this holiday?
activity_choices: Record silly and goofy greeting messages
activity_answer: Record silly and goofy greeting messages
follow_up_question: What would be the funniest voicemail greeting you could record?
